enabled.description:
Enable Horizontal POD autoscaling.
Note: Not recommended when using ACME certificates as certificate state is not shared.
enabled.title: enabled

extraEnv.description: additional environment variables to be added to the pods
extraEnv.title: extraEnv

extraEnvFrom.description: additional environment variables from ConfigMaps or Secrets
extraEnvFrom.title: extraEnvFrom

global.description: Global values are values that can be accessed from any chart or subchart by exactly the same name.
global.title: global

tag.description: Overrides the image tag whose default is the chart appVersion.
tag.title: tag

imagePullSecrets.description: If defined, uses a Secret to pull an image from a private Docker registry or repository.
imagePullSecrets.title: imagePullSecrets

annotations.description: Additional annotations for the Ingress resource
annotations.title: annotations

className.description: IngressClass that will be be used to implement the Ingress
className.title: className

enabled.description:
Enable ingress record generation.
Note: This is typically not needed as the reverse proxy handles TLS termination itself.
enabled.title: enabled

hosts.description:
cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: cluster-issuer-name
kubernetes.io/ingress.class: nginx
kubernetes.io/tls-acme: "true"
An array with hosts and paths
hosts.title: hosts

tls.description: An array with the tls configuration
tls.title: tls

nameOverride.description: Provide a name in place of `netbird-reverse-proxy`
nameOverride.title: nameOverride

accessModes.description: The desired access modes the volume should have
accessModes.title: accessModes

annotations.description: Annotations to be added to the PersistentVolumeClaim
annotations.title: annotations

enabled.description:
Enable persistent storage for TLS certificates.
Recommended when proxy.acmeCertificates=true to avoid re-requesting certificates on pod restarts.
enabled.title: enabled

existingClaim.description: Provide an existing PersistentVolumeClaim. If set, no new PVC will be created.
existingClaim.title: existingClaim

resources.description: Represents the minimum and maximum resources the volume should have
resources.title: resources

storageClassName.description: Name of the StorageClass required by the claim
storageClassName.title: storageClassName

podAnnotations.description: Annotations to be added to the pods
podAnnotations.title: podAnnotations

acmeCertificates.description:
Enable ACME (Let's Encrypt) certificate management (NB_PROXY_ACME_CERTIFICATES).
When enabled, certificates are stored in the directory specified by proxy.certificateDirectory.
Consider enabling persistence.enabled=true to avoid certificate loss on pod restarts.
acmeCertificates.title: acmeCertificates

address.description: Address the HTTPS proxy listener binds to (NB_PROXY_ADDRESS)
address.title: address

allowInsecure.description: Allow insecure (non-TLS) connections to upstream services (NB_PROXY_ALLOW_INSECURE)
allowInsecure.title: allowInsecure

existingSecret.description:
Name of an existing secret containing the proxy token.
If set, proxy.auth.token will be ignored.
The secret must contain a key named `token`.
existingSecret.title: existingSecret

token.description:
Proxy authentication token (NB_PROXY_TOKEN).
It is strongly recommended to use proxy.auth.existingSecret instead of providing the token here.
token.title: token

certificateDirectory.description: Directory where TLS certificates are stored (NB_PROXY_CERTIFICATE_DIRECTORY)
certificateDirectory.title: certificateDirectory

managementAddress.description: Address of the NetBird management server (NB_PROXY_MANAGEMENT_ADDRESS)
managementAddress.title: managementAddress

private.description: Whether this is a private proxy (NB_PROXY_PRIVATE)
private.title: private

replicaCount.description:
Number of replicas.
Note: When using ACME certificates (proxy.acmeCertificates=true), running more than one
replica is not recommended as certificate state is not shared between pods.
replicaCount.title: replicaCount

resources.description: Resource limits and requests for the controller pods.
resources.title: resources

revisionHistoryLimit.description: The number of old ReplicaSets to retain
revisionHistoryLimit.title: revisionHistoryLimit

route.description:
BETA: Configure the gateway routes for the chart here.
More routes can be added by adding a dictionary key like the 'main' route.
Being BETA this can/will change in the future without notice, do not use unless you want to take that risk
[[ref]](https://gateway-api.sigs.k8s.io/references/spec/#gateway.networking.k8s.io%2fv1alpha2)

additionalRules.description: Additional custom rules that can be added to the route
additionalRules.title: additionalRules

apiVersion.description: Set the route apiVersion, e.g. gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1 or gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1alpha2
apiVersion.title: apiVersion

filters.description: Filters define the filters that are applied to requests that match this rule.
filters.title: filters

httpsRedirect.description: adds a filter for redirecting to https (HTTP 301 Moved Permanently). To redirect HTTP traffic to HTTPS, you need to have a Gateway with both HTTP and HTTPS listeners. Matches and filters do not take effect if enabled. Ref. https://gateway-api.sigs.k8s.io/guides/http-redirect-rewrite/
httpsRedirect.title: httpsRedirect

kind.description:
Set the route kind
Valid options are GRPCRoute, HTTPRoute, TCPRoute, TLSRoute, UDPRoute
kind.title: kind

matches.description: define conditions used for matching the rule against incoming HTTP requests.
matches.title: matches

timeouts.description: defines the timeouts that can be configured for an HTTP request
timeouts.title: timeouts

route.title: route

securityContext.description:
container-level security context.
Note: The proxy needs to bind to ports 80 and 443 which may require elevated privileges
depending on your cluster configuration.
securityContext.title: securityContext

annotations.description: Additional annotations for the Service resource
annotations.title: annotations

port.description: Kubernetes service port for HTTP traffic
port.title: port

port.description: Kubernetes service port for HTTPS traffic
port.title: port

annotations.description: Annotations to add to the service account
annotations.title: annotations

create.description: Specifies whether a service account should be created
create.title: create

name.description:
The name of the service account to use.
If not set and create is true, a name is generated using the fullname template
name.title: name
